data.title: Ventilator Associated Pneumonia: Addition of Tobramycin Inhalation Antibiotic Treatment to Standard IV Antibiotic Treatment
data.brief_summary: This study evaluates the addition of tobramycin inhalation treatment to standard intravenous therapy in the treatment of ventilator associated pneumonia.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Mechanical ventilation 48 hours or more
* New or progressive radiologic pulmonary infiltrate

Together with at least two of the following three criteria (\< 24 h):

* temperature \>38°C
* leukocytosis \>12,000/mm3 or leucopenia \<4,000/mm3
* purulent respiratory secretions

data.title: A Double-Blind, Active-Controlled, Multiple-Ascending Dose, Phase 1b/2a Study of Aerosolized RSP-1502 Delivered Via the PARI LC Plus® Nebulizer in Subjects With Cystic Fibrosis and Chronic Pseudomonas Aeruginosa Lung Infection
data.brief_summary: A double-blind, active-controlled, multiple-ascending dose, safety study of aerosolized RSP-1502 in subjects with cystic fibrosis Pseudomonas aeruginosa lung infection.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Males or females aged ≥18 years of age for cohorts 1-4; males or females ≥12 years of age for cohort 5.

data.title: A Crossover, Cluster Randomised Controlled Trial of Selective Decontamination of the Digestive Tract in Intensive Care Unit Patients (SuDDICU)
data.brief_summary: Introduction- Hospital acquired infections (HAI) are a major cause of morbidity and mortality and increase health care costs. Critically ill patients are particularly susceptible to these infections and have an even higher mortality. One intervention that has gained much interest in the medical literature for reducing infection rates and deaths from HAIs is selective decontamination of the digestive tract (SDD). data.title: Early Discontinuation of Antibiotics for Unexplained Febrile Neutropenia: a Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial- EASE ANTIBIOTICS Pilot Trial
data.brief_summary: Infections are a common complication in patients with cancer. They are a significant cause of complications and death in this population. Patients with cancer and low neutrophil counts due to chemotherapy or disease often have a fever and receive antibiotic treatment. The optimal duration of this treatment is largely unknown.
